story.text: As an AI, I need to file every story under the epic it advances and brief bdo at that scale first — the arc, its context, and how each local piece glues in — because bdo wants to steer arcs of value, not approve tickets.

briefing.headline: Steer epics, not tickets
briefing.value: You said it directly: 'I want to see epic level ideas and context, not individual tasks or tickets.' From now on every item that reaches you is shown inside its arc — what the big idea is, why it exists, and how this specific piece glues into it — so a glance gives you the epic and a minute gives you the verdict.
briefing.why_now: Your last two stamps came with a rider: the tickets were briefed but the scale was wrong. You also granted the sizing rule that makes this workable: larger swaths of value at once, as long as the local pieces each glue into value and carry their own receipts.
briefing.if_accepted: Epics become the steering level: two already retro-cover everything built (epic.substrate, epic.owner-harness), new work files under an epic or says why it can't, and the inbox briefs arc-first everywhere.
briefing.if_rejected: Items stay ticket-scale and you keep reconstructing the big picture yourself on every visit.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low: epics are records and rendering — no gate logic, no truth moved. Retiring the layer is deleting files the log never depended on.
briefing.mechanism: Epic records in .ai-native/epics/ carry arc/context/value/horizon/pieces+glue; atoms file via incidence.serves or the epic's pieces list (settled atoms retro-file without re-judging — the epic points at them); CLI inbox and the web page group and brief epic-first; unfiled atoms still render.

_note: This file is the atom's birth record and content-hash source. It is never mutated: current verdicts and state are always the fold over .ai-native/log/ (D-5).
